description_long: This is a modern commercial cargo airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white, black, and red, featuring the SF Airlines livery and logo. The aircraft has a pair of swept-back low-mounted wings with underwing twin turbofan engines, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer marked with the number '72'. The fuselage includes a large cargo door on the left side, indicating its use for freight transport. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the flight crew. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of a medium-sized jet-powered cargo aircraft used in urban and intercity logistics.
description: A modern SF Airlines cargo airplane with swept low-mounted wings, twin underwing turbofan engines, and a prominent cargo door. The livery is white, black, and red with a conventional tail and forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical low-mounted wings with visible engines.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large cargo door, SF Airlines branding, and the full length of the fuselage and tail.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, engine placement, and the streamlined shape of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the SF Airlines logo, tail number, and the closed cargo door with engine detail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle for stability.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan underwing engines, one mounted beneath each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, implied by typical design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows for pilot visibility.
